title_english: The Tale of Two Bad Mice

pages.latin_text: Ecce domus pulchra! Mūrī rubrī et fenestrae albae.
pages.english_text: This is a beautiful dollhouse. It has red walls and white windows.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_004.jpg] The red brick dollhouse with white windows and muslin curtains.

pages.latin_text: Duae pūpae ibi habitābant: Lucinda et Iāna. Iāna coqua erat.
pages.english_text: Two dolls lived there: Lucinda and Jane. Jane was the cook.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_005.jpg] The two dolls standing by their packing case.

pages.latin_text: Cibus pulcher in mēnsā erat: perna, piscis, et lībum.
pages.english_text: There was pretty food on the table: a ham, a fish, and a pudding.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_006.jpg] Close-up of the dollhouse dining table laden with beautiful toy food.

pages.latin_text: Ōlim māne pūpae exiērunt. Domus tacita erat.
pages.english_text: One morning the dolls went out. The house was quiet.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_007.jpg] Tom Thumb popping his head out of the mouse-hole.

pages.latin_text: Tom Pollex mūs erat. Hunca Munca uxor eius erat.
pages.english_text: Tom Thumb was a mouse. Hunca Munca was his wife.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_008.jpg] Both mice peering out of their hole together.

pages.latin_text: Mūrēs ad domum rēpsērunt. Iānuam impulērunt!
pages.english_text: The mice crept up to the dollhouse. They pushed the door open!
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_009.jpg] The two mice creeping across the hearthrug toward the dollhouse.

pages.latin_text: Cēnāculum invēnērunt. Quantae epulae!
pages.english_text: They found the dining room. What a feast!
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_010.jpg] The mice peering into the dining room, eyes wide.

pages.latin_text: Tom Pollex pernam secāre temptāvit. Culter flexus est!
pages.english_text: Tom Thumb tried to cut the ham. The knife bent!
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_011.jpg] Tom Thumb pressing a lead knife into the ham.

pages.latin_text: Hunca Munca quoque temptāvit. Perna dūrissima erat!
pages.english_text: Hunca Munca tried too. The ham was very hard!
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_012.jpg] Hunca Munca standing on a chair, chopping at the ham.

pages.latin_text: Perna dē pātellā cecidit et sub mēnsam volūta est.
pages.english_text: The ham fell off the plate and rolled under the table.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_013.jpg] The ham rolling under the table.

pages.latin_text: Tom Pollex īrātus erat! Cibum batillō frēgit. Gypsum erat!
pages.english_text: Tom Thumb was angry! He smashed the food with a shovel. It was all plaster!
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_014.jpg] Tom Thumb smashing plates with tongs and shovel.

pages.latin_text: Piscem in ignem posuērunt. Nōn ārsit!
pages.english_text: They put the fish in the fire. It would not burn!
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_015.jpg] Hunca Munca pushing the fish into the paper fire.

pages.latin_text: Tom Pollex camīnum ascendit. Nūlla fūlīgō!
pages.english_text: Tom Thumb went up the chimney. There was no soot!
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_016.jpg] Tom Thumb looking out from the top of the chimney.

pages.latin_text: Hunca Munca vāscula invēnit. Sed vacua erant!
pages.english_text: Hunca Munca found little cans. But they were empty inside!
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_017.jpg] Hunca Munca turning canisters upside down.

pages.latin_text: Mūrēs valdē īrātī erant! Tom Pollex omnia per fenestram iēcit!
pages.english_text: The mice were very angry! Tom Thumb threw everything out the window!
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_018.jpg] Tom Thumb throwing clothes out the upstairs window.

pages.latin_text: Hunca Munca culcitam plūmeam domum tulit.
pages.english_text: Hunca Munca took the feather bed home to the mouse-hole.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_019.jpg] The mice carrying the bolster down the stairs.

pages.latin_text: Cūnās quoque cēpit, et caveam avium!
pages.english_text: She took a cradle and a birdcage too!
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_021.jpg] Hunca Munca carrying the cradle away.

pages.latin_text: Tum pūpae domum rediērunt. Quanta ruīna!
pages.english_text: Then the dolls came home. What a mess!
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_023.jpg] Lucinda and Jane in their wrecked dollhouse.

pages.latin_text: Sed mūrēs paenituit. Tom Pollex nummum invēnit et prō omnibus quae frēgerat solvit.
pages.english_text: But the mice were sorry. Tom Thumb found a coin and paid for everything he broke.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_028.jpg] The mice stuffing a sixpence into a stocking.

pages.latin_text: Et cotīdiē māne, Hunca Munca domum pūpārum verrit.
pages.english_text: And every morning, Hunca Munca sweeps the dollhouse clean.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_029.jpg] Hunca Munca with broom and dustpan at the dollhouse door.
